level_signals: 6+ years of experience in community operations, trust & safety operations, escalations, or related role
Founding member of a new pillar of the Operations organization
Compensation range $210K-$260K base, consistent with Senior IC at Anthropic

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 6+ years of experience in community operations, trust & safety operations, escalations, customer support escalations, or a related front-line operations role—ideally at a consumer or developer platform
must_haves.qualifier: ideally at a consumer or developer platform

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Direct, hands-on experience running incidents or escalations: you've been the person in the channel paging on-call, coordinating the partners, and writing the post-mortem
Sharp written communication—you can brief a senior leader in three sentences and document an incident so the next responder can pick it up cold
Comfort coordinating across functions in real time and influencing partners without positional authority
Familiarity with social listening tooling and an instinct for how issues spread across public social platforms
A pattern-detection mindset—you spot the second-order issue beneath the first-order complaint and surface it to the right people
Comfort with ambiguity and a bias toward action; this team is brand new and you'll be helping define what good looks like as you operate
Excitement about protecting Anthropic's reputation and relationships at a moment when public attention on AI has never been higher
Bachelor's degree or an equivalent combination of education, training, and/or experience

scope.surface: Community & Executive Escalations — social media monitoring, executive channel escalations, incident management, and brand reputation protection
primary_metrics_inferred: Post-incident report quality and pattern surfacing
Anthropic brand reputation health on public social channels
core_responsibilities: Own incidents and executive escalations end-to-end during coverage hours
Actively monitor public social channels for brand mentions, surfacing potential escalations and triaging them against defined criteria
Communicate crisply with internal stakeholders: brief executives on live situations, give Comms partners the facts they need, and keep incident channels useful and current
Write clear post-incident reports that capture what happened, what we did, and what should change
Help iterate on the team's playbooks, response templates, escalation criteria, and SOPs based on real cases
Spot patterns across escalations and surface them to the right partners

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and competitive ($210K-$260K base)
Founding member opportunity — high visibility and ownership of a new function
Clear scope: specific, well-defined responsibilities with real operational impact
Encourages applications even from candidates who don't meet every qualification
Explicit inclusion and diversity language in the posting
red_flags: Hybrid policy requires at least 25% office time in San Francisco — eliminates fully-remote candidates
Brand-new team with no established playbooks means candidate must build from scratch with limited support structure
All must-haves are framed under 'You may be a good fit if you have' — ambiguity about which are truly hard requirements vs. preferred

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range + years required)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated in JD; inferred from responsibilities)
location.cities_listed (inferred from HQ reference; not explicitly stated for this role in body)
equity_form (inferred from company benefits section mentioning 'optional equity donation matching')
ambiguities: All requirements are listed under 'You may be a good fit if you have:' rather than a 'Required' or 'Must have' section. This softens the language, but the combination of 6+ years minimum, specificity of skills, and logistics section ('Minimum years of experience: Years of experience required will correlate with the internal job level requirements') implies these are effectively hard requirements. Classified as hard accordingly.

Nice-to-haves section is empty because no items were explicitly labeled as 'preferred,' 'bonus,' or 'nice to have' in the JD. The platform qualifier ('ideally at a consumer or developer platform') was retained inline as a qualifier on the years_of_experience requirement rather than broken out as a separate soft requirement.
